snippets.title: Guillermo Rauch on agents and models
snippets.takeaway: React → Next.js → @aisdk is more relevant than ever, given the intense model competition landscape. Just today, GLM 5.2, an open model, surpassed Opus 4.8 in our Next.
snippets.summary: Guillermo Rauch (rauchg on X) shared 3 recent updates. React → Next.js → @aisdk is more relevant than ever, given the intense model competition landscape. Just today, GLM 5.2, an open model, surpassed Opus 4.8 in our Next.js Evals ( 🤯 But the world needs a practical solution for how to build and deploy agents. Just like React needed Next.js to solve the task of building an actual web application. And that's eve.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on agents and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: The past couple months we may be witnessing what the Applied AI layer will look like at scale. Despite some of the initial critique that this would just be a thin layer on the...
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 1 recent update. The past couple months we may be witnessing what the Applied AI layer will look like at scale. Despite some of the initial critique that this would just be a thin layer on the LLM, it’s turning out that actually driving agentic workflows in an enterprise is far more complex. And anywhere there’s complexity you generally gain a moat and value over time.

snippets.title: Garry Tan on models and costs
snippets.takeaway: We don’t care what your age is We care if you can build with craft and care And if you can make something people want Rough estimate on $ productivity lost by Fable 5 ban: $12M...
snippets.summary: Garry Tan (garrytan on X) shared 3 recent updates. We don’t care what your age is We care if you can build with craft and care And if you can make something people want Rough estimate on $ productivity lost by Fable 5 ban: $12M per hour Frontier AI-coding daily actives, mid-2026: 5M devs Fully-loaded cost: $90/hr Work routed to Fable in 48 hours: 17.8% Fable is on average ~15% more productive Effective throughput loss per dev = 17.8% × 15% ≈ 2.7% of output 2.

snippets.title: Zara Zhang on coding and product
snippets.takeaway: Don't use AI for writing until you develop your own taste and voice Using AI to write isn't inherently bad.
snippets.summary: Zara Zhang (zarazhangrui on X) shared 2 recent updates. Don't use AI for writing until you develop your own taste and voice Using AI to write isn't inherently bad. The danger is using AI to write before you've developed your taste for what is good content. If the AI produces slop, you won't even recognize it as slop Read a lot to figure out what good looks like. Write a lot to know what your voice sounds like.

snippets.title: Claude on coding and models
snippets.takeaway: Claude Design is in beta on all paid plans, on web and desktop. Give it a try: The redesigned editor is steadier for daily work.
snippets.summary: Claude (claudeai on X) shared 3 recent updates. Claude Design is in beta on all paid plans, on web and desktop. Give it a try: The redesigned editor is steadier for daily work. New layout controls let you drag, resize, and align elements directly on the canvas. Claude Design and Claude Code work together in both directions (rolling out today). Hand a design off to build, or start in Claude Code and sync design projects from your terminal.

snippets.title: GitHub’s COO Explains Why AI Hasn’t Replaced Developers
snippets.takeaway: Enterprise AI is moving from chat rollout toward agentic workflows, with token budgets, rollout speed, and workflow integration becoming the practical bottlenecks.
snippets.summary: I'm the head of tech consulting at Every, and I sat down with Kyle Daigle, the COO of GitHub, and talked to him about what is happening on the front lines of coding agents. So GitHub's always evolving as the industry and community does, but we're always sort of focused on, I need to make sure you, the dev, have what you need to be successful, you know, and then work with enterprises, you know, to make sure they have what they need to do at scale, which is usually a little bit different than what, you know, an...
